source_jobs.title: (Senior) Programme Manager
source_jobs.description:
Role

As (Senior) Programme Manager for SE3, you will play a defining role in bringing our autonomous systems and Spatial AI capability to market with European militaries and industry partners. You will develop commercial strategies, institutional relationships, and capture campaigns that turn SE3's technology into awarded programmes.

 - Lead programmes across Europe from bid/no-bid decision, capture, commercial bid development, contract negotiation, and award

 - Build and maintain senior institutional relationships, including representation of SE3 at industry events

 - Manage relationships with prime contractors and structure partnerships

 - Lead proposal development end-to-end from capture strategy to win

 - Develop the commercial response at the intersection of legal, finance and procurement, building robust commercial models that deliver on SE3's growth objectives



Your profile

 - A degree in business, engineering, political science, international relations, or an adjacent discipline; a postgraduate qualification is welcome

 - Professional experience across defence procurement, public-sector strategy consulting, business development in the public sector

 - Willingness to travel frequently across Europe and into other strategic markets where SE3 is pursuing opportunities

 - Confident operator across cultures, able to communicate and negotiate with senior stakeholders

 - Native-level German and full professional fluency in English, in both written and spoken form

Given the nature of SE3's work in the defence sector, candidates must be eligible to obtain and hold the security clearance required for this role.



What we value

 - Ownership: take responsibility for outcomes and remove obstacles to delivery without requiring direction.

 - Execution: convert discussions into clear decisions, decisions into deliverables.

 - High standards: hold yourself and the team to a consistently high standard of work.



Perks & benefits

 - 31 paid days off: 30 vacation days plus your birthday off

 - Well-being access: free membership to gyms, yoga and wellness through EGYM Wellpass

 - Team offsites: 2 company retreats per year
   
   

About SE3

SE3 Labs develops foundational Spatial AI technology for autonomous systems. The software processes sensor data in real time into a three-dimensional operational picture, synchronised across all platforms. Operators command the entire fleet using natural language. One operator, many systems, maximum effect.

We combine deep scientific expertise, grounded in over a decade of leading research in AI and computer vision, with a clear focus on building systems that are proven in real-world environments.



Equal opportunity

SE3 is an equal-opportunity employer committed to building a diverse and inclusive team. All qualified candidates will be considered for employment without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, or any other characteristic protected under applicable law.

source_jobs.title: Drone Test & Field Engineer
source_jobs.description:
🚀 JOIN SE3 LABS AS DRONE TEST & FIELD ENGINEER

Help us get our autonomous systems airborne and pushed to their limits. Turn advanced designs into robust, field-proven, real-world solutions. Early team, deep tech, high impact.



ABOUT SE3

SE3 Labs is building Spatial AI by combining 3D computer vision with large language models (LLMs). We believe the ability to reason about and act within physical space is foundational to unlocking the next generation of automation, from construction to smart infrastructure, and ultimately, defense.

Now, we’re building the physical platform to bring Spatial AI to life. That means high-performance, real-world robotic systems (starting with UAVs) and we’re looking for a hands-on operator who’s ready to fly, demonstrate and perfect these machines with us.



ABOUT THE ROLE

As our Drone Test & Field Engineer, you’ll play a key role in getting and keeping our fleet of autonomous drones in the air. Working as part of our development team, you will bring new drones to life where hardware and software come together and test their capabilities systematically in the real world. Your contribution will determine how well we understand our technology and define the path of our future development.




WHAT YOU’LL WORK ON

 - Integrate & Checkout: Work hand-in-hand with our hardware & software developers and technicians to complete integration and get new UAVs to flight readiness.

 - Launch and Fly: Operate and fine-tune drones from backpackable quadcopters to three meter wingspan fixed-wings (eVTOL & catapult launch).

 - Test Strategy: You own qualification and development flight testing, from structure and organization over execution in the field to data processing and documentation.

 - Improve & Suggest: Spot bugs and design flaws, propose & evaluate improvements, and help us move faster and build better.

 - Demonstrate & Train: Introduce our systems to partners and end users on our test-base and in the field across Europe.
   


YOUR PROFILE

 - You’ve completed a Bachelors in Aerospace Engineering, Robotics or another relevant technical field

 - You bring hands-on experience assembling, tuning and operating drones: 3+ years professionally or equivalent in racing / competition / enthusiast projects

 - You are intimate with Q Ground Control, Ardupilot or PX4

 - You bring a responsible and structured approach to mission-planning, airspace coordination, etc.

 - You’re skilled in troubleshooting drones and electronics

 - You care about precision, quality and a systematic problem-solving approach

 - You are proactive, willing to improvise and have a strong sense of ownership

 - Conversational English is a must; German is a strong plus

 - Driving license class B, BE is a plus




ESPECIALLY VALUED BONUS SKILLS

 - Data analysis skills (log interpretation, root cause-, frequency domain analysis)

 - You are familiar with using a command line and basic scripting

 - Experience running environmental tests like vibration, EMI or thermal, ideally MIL-Spec

 - Regulatory experience (EASA SORA or similar frameworks)

 - Good understanding of sensors, signal chains, and testing setups

 - Familiarity with CAD and 3D printing

 - Completed military basic training and/or understanding of army processes




PERKS & BENEFITS

 - Make your passion your job: If you love drones, you will relish this role

 - AI-boosted workplace: We empower every employee with best-in-class AI tools and agents, giving you unlimited access to the latest advancements and a true productivity edge

 - Real Ownership: Get early, real stock options to participate directly in SE3’s growth

 - 31 Paid Days Off: 30 vacation days plus your birthday off

 - Well-being Access: Free membership to gyms, yoga, and wellness through EGYM Wellpass

 - Team Offsites: We go on 2 company retreats per year, our last ones were in Italy, Greece and Spain

 - Mentorship: Work closely with experienced engineers and get paired with a professional mento

source_jobs.title: Government Relations Intern
source_jobs.description:
Role
As Government Relations Intern at SE3, you will support and expand SE3's engagement with governmental institutions across Europe. You will contribute to strategic initiatives across Germany, the United Kingdom, the Nordics and Ukraine. This is a role with real responsibility from day one, at a company building some of the most advanced autonomous systems in Europe.

 - Support the development and execution of country-specific government engagement strategies across SE3's priority markets

 - Analyse national and European defence procurement frameworks, public funding instruments and regulatory developments, and translate insights into actionable strategic recommendations

 - Contribute to national and international public funding applications

 - Prepare briefings, background memos and strategic materials for senior-level meetings with public sector representatives

Your profile

 - Currently enrolled in a Master's degree in political science, international relations, public affairs, law or a related field

 - First-hand experience within a governmental institution e.g. federal ministry, parliament, EU body or international organisation

 - Strong understanding of European political institutions and defence policy landscapes

 - Native-level German and English (C2); French is a strong plus

 - Proactive, structured and comfortable operating in a fast-paced, high-responsibility environment; prior exposure to technology policy, defence innovation or entrepreneurial environments is a plus

Minimum 4 months, 6 months strongly preferred.



What we value

 - Ownership: take responsibility for outcomes and remove obstacles to delivery without requiring direction

 - Execution: convert discussions into clear decisions, decisions into deliverables

 - High standards: hold yourself and the team to a consistently high standard of work



Perks & benefits

 - 31 paid days off: 30 vacation days plus your birthday off

 - Well-being access: free membership to gyms, yoga and wellness through EGYM Wellpass

 - Team offsites: 2 company retreats per year



About SE3
SE3 Labs develops foundational Spatial AI technology for autonomous systems. The software processes sensor data in real time into a three-dimensional operational picture, synchronised across all platforms. Operators command the entire fleet using natural language. One operator, many systems, maximum effect.

source_jobs.title: Technical Project Manager
source_jobs.description:
Role
As Technical Project Manager at SE3, you will own end-to-end delivery across defence R&D programmes involving engineering teams, consortium partners and public-sector customers. You will drive execution from kickoff to final acceptance, maintain clarity on priorities and commitments across all workstreams, and ensure risks are visible and managed before they affect outcomes. This is a role with genuine delivery ownership and direct exposure to senior stakeholders at a company building some of the most advanced autonomous systems in Europe.

 - Own project delivery from kickoff to final acceptance across scope, milestones, budget and quality

 - Maintain the project roadmap and keep engineering, commercial and partner workstreams aligned on priorities and commitments

 - Build and run the project operating rhythm: planning cadence, decision logs, change control and structured follow-through on actions and owners

 - Represent SE3 in customer, partner and consortium meetings with clear, credible communication

 - Identify and manage risks and dependencies proactively, bringing structured options and trade-offs to programme leadership before issues affect delivery

 - Develop governance frameworks and delivery playbooks that allow SE3 to run multiple concurrent programmes with consistency and speed



Your profile

 - A degree in Industrial Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, Computer Science or an adjacent MINT discipline

 - 3+ years of professional experience managing complex, multi-stakeholder technical programmes; a consulting background in defence or regulated environments is strongly preferred

 - Hands-on experience with defence or public-sector programmes, EU-funded R&D, consortium structures

 - Strong documentation skills

 - Native-level German and full professional fluency in English, written and spoken

Given the nature of SE3's work in the defence sector, candidates must be eligible to obtain and hold the security clearance required for this role.



What we value

 - Ownership: take responsibility for outcomes and remove obstacles to delivery without requiring direction

 - Execution: convert discussions into clear decisions, decisions into deliverables

 - High standards: hold yourself and the team to a consistently high standard of work



Perks & benefits

 - 31 paid days off: 30 vacation days plus your birthday off

source_jobs.title: Senior Perception Engineer - VIO / SLAM / Sensor Fusion
source_jobs.description:
🚀 JOIN SE3 LABS AS SENIOR PERCEPTION ENGINEER

Own the spatial state-estimation layer of our autonomy platform. Build the perception systems that let drones understand where they are, what they see, and how to operate when GPS, lighting, timing, and the real world get difficult.

 


ABOUT SE3

SE3 Labs is building Spatial AI by combining 3D computer vision with large language models. We believe the ability to reason about and act within physical space is foundational to unlocking the next generation of automation, from construction to smart infrastructure, and ultimately, defence.

Now, we’re bringing Spatial AI into the sky through high-performance UAVs that can perceive, localize, coordinate, and act in the real world. This requires perception systems that are not just accurate in a notebook, but robust on real sensors, on real compute, in real missions.

 


ABOUT THE ROLE

As Senior Perception Engineer - VIO / SLAM / Sensor Fusion, you will own core parts of the real-time perception and localization stack for our autonomous systems. You will develop and deploy the algorithms that let our platforms estimate motion, maintain pose, build spatial understanding, and operate reliably in degraded or GPS-denied environments.

You will work across visual-inertial odometry, SLAM, sensor fusion, calibration, time synchronization, mapping, and real-time systems. Your work will sit at the center of our autonomy platform: connecting cameras, IMUs, GNSS, onboard compute, control systems, payloads, and Spatial AI models into a coherent understanding of the world.

This is a hands-on engineering role. You will design algorithms, write production code, analyze logs, debug sensor issues, validate performance in simulation and flight, and work closely with robotics software, embedded, hardware, and field teams to turn perception research into fieldable capability.

 


WHAT YOU’LL WORK ON

 - Continuously push the boundary of what's possible: Improve state estimation capabilities in very difficult field conditions during heavy rain, snow, fog, in the night, under strong vibration, motion blur, imperfect calibration and sensor degradation. Utilize improved fusion, novel deep learning methods and our data engine.

 - VIO & State Estimation: Build, tune, and deploy robust visual-inertial odometry and state-estimation pipelines for robotics systems operating in dynamic, outdoor, and GPS-challenged environments. Initial focus is on UAVs, but we want to build algorithms that work well for any robotics system.

 - Sensor Fusion: Fuse and synchronize data from cameras, IMUs, magnetometers, barometers, and other onboard sensors into reliable pose, orientation, uncertainty estimates, and spatial context for downstream autonomy.

 - SLAM & Re-localization: Build mapping, loop closure, re-localization, and map-based navigation capabilities that let our systems maintain spatial understanding across missions, environments, and degraded sensor conditions.

 - Calibration & Timing: Own intrinsic, extrinsic, and temporal calibration workflows. Diagnose drift, latency, frame drops, sync issues, rolling-shutter effects, vibration, and sensor degradation.

 - Real-Time Deployment: Optimize perception pipelines for onboard compute, including Nvidia Jetson-class platforms, low-power edge CPUs/GPUs, and resource-constrained robotic systems.

 - Validation & Regression: Build replayable log pipelines, evaluation datasets, test metrics, and automated regression tests to measure localization accuracy, robustness, latency, and failure modes on a huge company-internal benchmark.

 - Field Debugging: Analyze real mission logs, reproduce failures, isolate root causes, and improve the system until it works not only in ideal conditions, but in the field.

 - Autonomy Integration: Define clean interfaces to planning, control, fleet coordination, operator UI, and Spatial AI systems so that perception outputs are usable, reliable, and explainable.

 - Technical Leadership: Me

source_jobs.title: Drone Assembly Working Student/ Freelancer
source_jobs.description:
🚀 JOIN SE3 LABS AS DRONE TEST & FIELD INTERN

Help us get our autonomous systems airborne and pushed to their limits. Turn advanced designs into robust, field-proven, real-world solutions. Early team, deep tech, high impact.




ABOUT SE3

SE3 Labs is building Spatial AI by combining 3D computer vision with large language models (LLMs). We believe the ability to reason about and act within physical space is foundational to unlocking the next generation of automation, from construction to smart infrastructure, and ultimately, defence.

Now, we’re building the physical platform to bring Spatial AI to life. That means high-performance, real-world robotic systems (starting with UAVs) and we’re looking for a hands-on operator who’s ready to fly, demonstrate and perfect these machines with us.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As our Drone Assembly Working Student/ Freelancer, you’ll play a key role in getting and keeping our fleet of autonomous drones in the air. Working as part of our development team, you will bring new drones to life where hardware and software come together and test their capabilities systematically in the real world. Your contribution will determine how well we understand our technology and define the path of our future development.




WHAT YOU’LL WORK ON

 - Integrate & Checkout: Work hand-in-hand with our hardware & software developers and technicians to complete integration and get new UAVs to flight readiness.

 - Fly, Test-Fly, Deploy and Fly: Flash, tune and operate drones from backpackable quadcopters to multi-meter wingspan fixed-wings.

 - Test & Feedback: You drive qualification and development flight testing in the field

 - Mentorship: Work closely with experienced engineers and get paired with a professional mentor to support your growth




OUR PROCESS

 1. Intro Call (20 min): Let’s get to know each other (conducted by Michael, our Chief of Staff)

 2. Technical Interview (45 min): (with Francisco, our Drone Software & Test Specialist )

source_jobs.title: Mechanical Design Engineer, Unmanned Systems
source_jobs.description:
Own the physical integration of our autonomy platform into drones, payloads and ground systems. Your work defines the fieldability, performance and production quality of our core hardware.



ABOUT SE3

SE3 Labs is building Spatial AI by combining 3D computer vision with large language models (LLMs). We believe the ability to reason about and act within physical space is foundational to unlocking the next generation of automation, from construction to smart infrastructure, and with a strong focus on defense.

Now, we’re building the physical platform to bring Spatial AI to life. That means high-performance, real-world robotic systems and we’re looking for a skilled, hands-on engineer who can turn strong concepts into robust products.



ABOUT THE ROLE

As Mechanical Design Engineer, Unmanned Systems, you’ll design the mechanisms, enclosures and physical integration of our on-board systems into various UAV platforms. From concept trade-off to CAD to flight test and on to production introduction, you will bring electronics, airframes, payloads and ground systems together into flying, connected and advanced multi-domain systems.

You’ll report directly to our Hardware Lead and collaborate most closely with electronics developers and prototyping team. SE3 sets no bounds on collaboration so you will engage with our customer-facing colleagues and AI software developers to realize an ambitious vision of whole systems with intelligent drones at the center. Mechanical integration will be your domain, and you’ll be trusted to own it independently.

 


WHAT YOU’LL WORK ON

 - Requirements & Concepts: Translate mission-needs into hardware requirements and trade-off design concepts to meet them

 - Design & Engineer: Design and refine sealed avionics enclosures, cooling concepts, interfaces, connection and release mechanisms, packaging and integration into UAVs and ground systems such as charging-, control- and launch platforms

 - Prototype & Iterate: Build, rework, and test hardware with speed and precision, in simulation, test rigs and in flight

 - Validate & Field-Test: Analyze and improve designs via simulation and real-world testing up until and including exercises with partners and armed forces

 - Document and Industrialize: Make your work the lasting foundation of our tech and bring it into thousands of customers’ hands in collaboration with our manufacturing partner network

 


YOUR PROFILE

 - M.Sc. in Aerospace, Mechanical Engineering or a related field

 - You bring 2+ years of hands-on experience in hardware design and development, ideally including mechanism design from concept to tested (sub-) system

 - You’re highly proficient in 3D modelling (CAD, preferably Onshape) and creating production-quality drawings

 - You’re familiar with selection and sizing of structural elements, fasteners, interfaces and thermal paths

 - You understand kinematics, packaging and electro-mechanical systems

 - You know when a simple answer is the right answer, and when a design needs deeper analysis, precision and careful consideration

 - You speak fluent English; German is a plus

 - Most importantly, you’re structured, fast-moving, and mission-driven. You don’t wait for perfection. You iterate until it works. You love building things that last.

 


ESPECIALLY VALUED BONUS SKILLS

 - Experience in designing lightweight electronic enclosures for harsh conditions

 - You’ve independently prototyped and tested mechanisms and seen them through refinement cycles into production

 - Experience with ISO GD&T, DFM/DFA, FEA interpretation and material selection

 - Thermal design and analysis for compact electronics or similar systems

 - Hardware testing & qualification experience (ideally including MIL-STD thermal, vibration, etc.)

 - Skilled in designing fixtures and test-rigs

 - Familiarity with light UAVs or aircraft systems or defense equipment

 


PERKS & BENEFITS

 - Work Environment to Excel at: Find a high-quality and modern toolse

source_jobs.title: Founding Drone & Avionics Technician
source_jobs.description:
🚀 JOIN SE3 LABS AS FOUNDING DRONE & AVIONICS TECHNICIAN

Help us get our autonomous systems airborne. You’ll own electrical integration from prototype to flight. Early team, deep tech, high impact.




ABOUT SE3

SE3 Labs is building Spatial AI by combining 3D computer vision with large language models (LLMs). We believe the ability to reason about and act within physical space is foundational to unlocking the next generation of automation, from construction to smart infrastructure, and ultimately, defense.

Now, we’re building the physical platform to bring Spatial AI to life. That means high-performance, real-world robotic systems (starting with UAVs) and we’re looking for a hands-on operator who’s ready to bring these machines into the real world.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As our Founding Drone & Avionics Technician, you’ll play a key role in getting our fleet of autonomous drones ready for flight. Working closely with our engineering team, you’ll establish our professional-grade avionics shop, build harnesses and integration workflows, and ensure every system is measured, tested, and flight-proven. You won’t just follow instructions, you’ll improve the way we work on your initiative and directly with our engineering team.




WHAT YOU’LL WORK ON

 - Wire & Build: Assemble and test prototype avionics units, including harnesses and electrical subsystems for UAVs

 - Integrate & Checkout: Install, measure, and verify electronics inside flight vehicles, ensuring robust, repeatable, and high-quality integration

 - Own the Shop: Set up, organize, and maintain our avionics workspace by selecting quality tools and building out a scalable integration process

 - Improve & Suggest: Spot design flaws, propose improvements, and help us move faster and build better

 - Test & Feedback: Support qualification and development testing, from vibration to thermal tests, and loop feedback back to engineering




YOUR PROFILE

 - You’ve completed a technical apprenticeship or training in a relevant field, e.g., Fluggeräteelektroniker, Elektroniker, or you’re EASA-certified

 - You bring 3+ years of hands-on experience assembling wiring harnesses and electronics, ideally in aerospace or high-reliability systems

 - You’re proficient in soldering wires, connectors, and PCBs, and you know your way around aerospace crimping and wire tools

 - You’re comfortable using electronic diagnostic tools and have a solid grasp of troubleshooting workflows

 - You care about precision, build quality, and structure. You know that great systems start with clean, reliable integration

 - You bring a proactive, organized approach and a deep sense of ownership. You don't just build, you improve

 - Conversational English is a must; German is a strong plus




ESPECIALLY VALUED BONUS SKILLS

 - Experience with sensors, signal chains, and testing setups

 - Operating or tuning drones (and the scars to prove it)

 - Familiarity with CAD, PLM, or MES systems

 - Experience running environmental tests like vibration or thermal




PERKS & BENEFITS

 - Get Certified: We'll train you to become a certified drone pilot

 - 31 Paid Days Off: 30 vacation days plus your birthday off

 - Well-being Access: Free membership to gyms, yoga, and wellness through EGYM Wellpass

 - Team Offsites: We go on 2 company retreats per year, our last ones were in Italy and Greece

 - Mentorship: Work closely with experienced engineers and get paired with a professional mentor to support your growth




OUR PROCESS

 1. Intro Call (20 min): Let’s get to know each other (conducted by Michael, our Chief of Staff)

 2. Technical Interview (45 min): (with Lukas, our Hardware Lead)

 3. Onsite Trial/Case (1.5 hrs): We’ll build or test something together, just like on the job (together with our Hardware Team)

 4. Team Dinner/Lunch: See if it’s a culture fit on both sides

source_jobs.title: Join Our Talent Pool
source_jobs.description:
JOIN OUR TALENT POOL

If you don’t see a current opening that fits your background, but you’re interested in working with SE3 Labs in the future, we invite you to join our Talent Pool. It’s the best way to stay connected and be considered for upcoming opportunities.




WHAT IS THE TALENT POOL?

The SE3 Labs Talent Pool is a group of people who want to stay on our radar for future roles. It helps us connect with individuals who share our curiosity, values, and passion for building new technologies. When new positions become available, we look to our Talent Pool first.




HOW DOES IT WORK?

Our Talent Pool is open to everyone. You can sign up at any time, and your profile will remain active for future searches. We regularly review submissions and reach out when there’s a match between your experience and our hiring needs.




WILL I HEAR ABOUT NEW SE3 LABS ROLES EARLY?

Often, yes. We contact people in our Talent Pool before we post roles publicly. Joining gives you an early look at new opportunities and projects that fit your skills and interests.




DO I NEED TO BE AN ENGINEER TO JOIN?

No. Our work spans a wide variety of disciplines, and we welcome people from different backgrounds and areas of expertise. Whether you’re early in your career or highly experienced, you’re encouraged to register your interest.



CAN I SPEAK WITH SOMEONE ON THE TEAM?

We receive many applications and expressions of interest, so we can’t speak with everyone individually. If we see strong alignment, we’ll reach out directly to arrange a conversation.



HOW TO JOIN

Click “Apply Here”, upload your resume, and share a few details about your background and interests. Once submitted, you’ll be part of our Talent Pool and we’ll be in touch when something relevant comes up.

source_jobs.title: UAV Test Pilot
source_jobs.description:
UAV TEST PILOT

Help us get our autonomous systems airborne and pushed to their limits. Turn advanced designs into robust, field-proven, real-world solutions. Early team, deep tech, high impact.



ABOUT SE3

SE3 Labs is building Spatial AI by combining 3D computer vision with large language models (LLMs). We believe the ability to reason about and act within physical space is foundational to unlocking the next generation of automation, from construction to smart infrastructure, and ultimately, defense.

Now, we’re building the physical platform to bring Spatial AI to life. That means high-performance, real-world robotic systems (starting with UAVs) and we’re looking for a hands-on operator who’s ready to fly, perfect and demonstrate these machines with us.



ABOUT THE ROLE

As our UAV Test Pilot, you’ll be the central operator flying our entire fleet of autonomous drones. Working as part of our development team, you will be the first to operate new drone and payload prototypes and evaluate them in demanding real-world conditions. Your contribution will determine how well we understand our technology and define the path of our future development.




WHAT YOU’LL WORK ON

 - Prepare & Launch: Integrate and tune new airframes and payloads with the team and bring systems to flight readiness

 - Fly & Evaluate: Run structured tests across quadcopters, up to three-meter wingspan fixed-wings, and eVTOL platforms

 - Stress the System: Execute realistic field scenarios and identify performance limits, failure modes, and reliability issues

 - Improve the Product: Spot bugs and design flaws, and work with engineers to improve performance and robustness

 - Demonstrate & Train: Demonstrate our systems to partners and end users on our test-base and in the field across Europe.



YOUR PROFILE

 - You’ve completed a technical apprenticeship or training in a relevant technical field

 - You have substantial hands-on experience assembling, tuning, repairing, and flying drones, whether professionally, in military service, or through serious FPV / builder / enthusiast projects.

 - You are intimate with Q Ground Control, Ardupilot or PX4

 - You bring a diligent and structured approach to flying

 - You are comfortable travelling, working outdoors in all weather, and operating in the field

 - You’re skilled in soldering wires and troubleshooting drones

 - You deliver precision and a systematic approach to communication and problem-solving

 - You are proactive, willing to improvise and have a strong sense of ownership

 - Conversational English is a must; German is a strong plus

 - Driving license class B, BE is a plus



ESPECIALLY VALUED BONUS SKILLS

 - Completed military training and/or understanding of army processes

 - Regulatory experience (EASA SORA or similar frameworks)

 - Hands-on repair or tinkering on electronics, radio equipment etc.

 - Experience running environmental tests like vibration, EMI or thermal, ideally MIL-Spec

 - Familiarity with CAD and 3D printing



PERKS & BENEFITS

 - Make your passion your job: If you love drones, you will relish this role

 - AI-boosted workplace: We empower every employee with best-in-class AI tools and agents, giving you unlimited access to the latest advancements and a true productivity edge

 - Real Ownership: Get early, real stock to participate directly in SE3’s growth

 - 31 Paid Days Off: 30 vacation days plus your birthday off

 - Well-being Access: Free membership to gyms, yoga, and wellness through EGYM Wellpass

 - Team Offsites: We go on 2 company retreats per year, our last ones were in Italy, Greece and Spain

 - Mentorship: Work closely with experienced engineers and get paired with a professional mentor to support your growth




OUR PROCESS

 1. Intro Call (20 min): Let’s get to know each other (conducted by Michael, our Chief of Staff)

 2. Technical Interview (45 min): A deeper dive into your goals, problem-solving approach and technical understanding (with Francis

source_jobs.title: Office Manager
source_jobs.description:
About SE3
SE3 Labs builds Spatial AI by combining 3D computer vision and large language models (LLMs). Ultimately, our AI will solve the spatial Turing test: It will be able to answer any question about 3D data that a human analyst could answer. Spatial AI will unlock tremendous value in industries like construction, smart infrastructure, smart factories, and defense. As our long-term vision, our spatial AI is a crucial building block for enabling LLM-controlled humanoid robots.

 - Our team consists of computer vision experts and repeat founders, who have worked with Elon Musk at Tesla and at Nvidia, KPMG, Bosch, and Artisense. Daniel is the president of the European Computer Vision Association.

 - Within our first year, we have secured mid-six-digit revenue and aim for seven-digits in 2025.

 - We are a dual-use tech company selling to civil customers and the German armed forces. We will expand internationally in the next six months.

About the Role
As Office Manager at SE3 Labs, you will be the operational backbone that keeps our fast-moving company running smoothly. You will own essential administrative processes, support leadership in day-to-day operations, and ensure that the team can work with focus and speed. Your work directly contributes to building one of Europe’s most ambitious AI companies.
You will work closely with Michael (Chief of Staff) and Lukas (Founder & CEO), gaining insight into how a deep-tech company operates from the inside.

Your Responsibilities

 - Own daily administrative workflows: including invoice and document management, vendor coordination, and handling internal approvals.

 - Coordinate logistics between our Munich office and testing facilities: ensuring smooth operational processes for hardware, equipment, and prototypes.

 - Support our R&D grant reporting: preparing timesheets, collecting team input, and helping maintain clean compliance documentation.

 - Plan and execute team events and offsites: play an active organisational part for the team culture by planning and organising the team events

 - Lead our merchandise initiatives: from new drops, supplier management and inventory handling, you make sure that our employees represent SE3

 - Serve as a trusted partner to leadership: helping the CEO and Chief of Staff run an organized, high-performance company.

What You Bring

 - Must have: Native German and strong conversational English.

 - Exceptional attention to detail and a structured, reliable way of solving problems.

 - A hands-on execution mindset: you enjoy getting things done and keeping core processes running.

 - Proactiveness and ownership: you see what needs to be done and drive it end-to-end without being asked twice.

 - A positive, energetic attitude and the ability to work in a dynamic, fast-growing scale-up environment.

Perks & Benefits

 - 31 days of paid leave: We offer 30 days of annual leave, plus an extra day off to celebrate your birthday.

 - Team activities: We organize at least 2x company offsite per year. The last one was at ECCV in Milan and the next one will be in Greece.

 - Fitness and Well-Being: Through our partner E-GYM Wellpass, we offer you access to a comprehensive fitness program — including premium gyms, climbing, and much more.

Process

 1. Intro Call (virtual, 30 minutes with Michael, Chief of Staff): This is our chance to get to know each other. We’ll discuss your background, your motivations, and why you’re interested in SE3 Labs. You’ll also learn more about our mission, the role, and the team.

 2. 2nd Interview (virtual, 30 minutes with Lukas, CEO): During this call, we dive a little deeper on the role and our CEO also wants to get to know you personally

 3. Onsite visit: Here, we will go through some responsibilities together as we want to understand how you approach them

 4. Dinner/lunch (in person): We would love to get to know you in person and tell you about how we work at SE3.

source_jobs.title: Founding Engineer 3D Computer Vision & AI
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SE3

SE3 Labs builds Spatial AI by combining 3D computer vision and large language models (LLMs). Ultimately, our AI will solve the spatial Turing test: It will be able to answer any question about 3D data that a human analyst could answer. Spatial AI will unlock tremendous value in industries like construction, smart infrastructure, smart factories, and defense. As our long-term vision, our spatial AI is a crucial building block for enabling LLM-controlled humanoid robots.

 - Our team consists of computer vision experts and repeat founders, who have worked with Elon Musk at Tesla and at Nvidia, KPMG, Bosch, and Artisense. Daniel is the president of the European Computer Vision Association.

 - Within our first year, we have secured mid-six-digit revenue and aim for seven-digits in 2025.

 - We are a dual-use tech company selling to civil customers and the German armed forces. We will expand internationally in the next six months.

 - We just raised a heavily over-subscribed financing round from Europe's best VCs (Midas List Europe https://www.forbes.com/lists/midas-europe/) and world-class angel investors (unicorn founders, Sequoia scout, Princeton professor, etc.). We are part of EWOR.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As the Founding Engineer 3D Computer Vision & AI, you will work closely with Lukas (CEO) and Simon (CTO) to build an AI-first perception and robotics company, similar to Tesla's Autopilot Team.




WHAT YOU MIGHT WORK ON

 - Develop and train new network architectures

 - Optimize network deployment on Nvidia Jetsons

 - Build our data engine using local and cloud compute

 - Combine existing LLMs with 3D computer vision to improve SpatialGPT

 - Fine-tune LLMs for better performance on 3D-related tasks

 - Integrate networks into real-time systems on drones




WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

 - Strong general software engineering skills

 - Theoretical and practical experience with deep learning

 - Theoretical and practical experience with 3D computer vision

 - SE3 strives to be the place where high-potential individuals can do their best work. We value quick learning and grit just as much as skill and experience.




PERKS & BENEFITS

 - Company equity: We offer above-average equity compensation (real equity, no virtual shares).

 - 31 days of paid leave: We offer 30 days of annual leave, plus an extra day off to celebrate your birthday.

 - Team activities: We organize at least 2x company offsite per year. The last one was at ECCV in Milan and the next one will be in Greece.

 - Fitness and Well-Being: Through our partner E-GYM Wellpass, we offer you access to a comprehensive fitness program — including premium gyms, climbing, and much more.




PROCESS

 1. Intro Call (virtual, 30 minutes): This is our chance to get to know each other. We’ll discuss your background, your motivations, and why you’re interested in SE3 Labs. You’ll also learn more about our mission, the role, and the team.

 2. LeetCode Interview (flexible, ~60 minutes): While LeetCode isn’t perfect, it will help us understand your approach to problem-solving and coding.

 3. Theory Interview (flexible, ~30 minutes): We will discuss fundamental concepts in 3D computer vision and AI.

 4. Dinner/lunch (in person): We would love to get to know you in person and tell you about how we work at SE3.

source_jobs.title: Embedded Software / Firmware Engineer
source_jobs.description:
EMBEDDED SOFTWARE / FIRMWARE ENGINEER

Own the low-level software layer of our autonomy platform across drones, payload and ground systems. Your work defines the fieldability, robustness and performance of our core product.



ABOUT SE3

SE3 Labs is building Spatial AI by combining 3D computer vision with large language models (LLMs). We believe the ability to reason about and act within physical space is foundational to unlocking the next generation of automation, from construction to smart infrastructure, and ultimately, defense.

Now, we’re building the physical platform to bring Spatial AI to life. That means high-performance, real-world robotic systems (starting with UAVs) and we’re looking for a skilled, hands-on embedded engineer who’s ready to power the foundation of these systems and have them communicate, update and perform reliably in the real world.



ABOUT THE ROLE

As Embedded Software / Firmware Engineer, you’ll own the low-level software foundation across our embedded platforms - from bring-up and driver development to deployment tooling and in-field update infrastructure. You’ll work across diverse compute platforms, enabling new sensors, cameras, AI accelerators and networked peripherals and turning prototypes into robust, fieldable systems.

This is a hands-on, on-site role in our prototyping and test space, with optional travel for field trials and partner testing. You’ll work closely with our hardware design and testing teams while collaborating with our world-class AI software team to ship vertically integrated solutions fast. Your contributions will directly shape system architecture and hardware design, and the systems you support will move quickly from bench to real-world testing and front-line deployment at scale reshaping Europe's defense capability.



WHAT YOU’LL WORK ON

 - Requirements & Bring-Up: Translate system needs into low-level software architecture and bring new boards and subsystems to life across Jetson, Arm and STM32 platforms

 - Drivers & Interfaces: Develop and maintain drivers and hardware-facing software for sensors, cameras, AI accelerators and communication networks

 - Kernel & System Layer: Work across embedded Linux, kernel and device-tree configuration, BSP-level integration and board bring-up to create stable, maintainable foundations

 - Flash, Boot & Deploy: Build and refine flashing, booting and serial deployment tooling for development, production, update and recovery workflows

 - Troubleshoot & Iterate: Debug on the bench, isolate failures fast, improve performance and stability, iterate until systems are ready for field use

 - Integrate & Maintain: Support system integration across aircraft platforms, work closely with hardware engineers and continuously improve performance, reliability and long-term maintainability




YOUR PROFILE

 - M.Sc. or higher in Computer Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Robotics, Embedded Systems or a related field

 - You bring 2+ years of hands-on experience in low-level software or firmware development for embedded systems; we’re equally interested and will reward more senior engineers who want to work deeply hands-on

 - You’re strong in C and Python and comfortable shipping real systems on embedded Linux in robotics, automotive or other hardware-adjacent environments

 - You have practical experience with hardware interfaces and protocols such as I2C, SPI, CAN, Ethernet and MIPI-based camera interfaces.

 - You’re comfortable with board bring-up, serial consoles, flashing workflows, boot issues, hardware-near debugging and systematic troubleshooting on real hardware

 - You know how to make software not just functional, but deployable, maintainable and robust under real-world conditions

 - You know how to validate low-level software on real hardware through structured debugging, integration testing and release-oriented validation.

 - Most importantly, you’re structured, fast-moving and mission-driven. You don’t wait for perfect

source_jobs.title: (Senior) Technical Field Engineer — Robotics Integration & Deployment
source_jobs.description:
🚀 JOIN SE3 LABS AS (SENIOR) TECHNICAL FIELD ENGINEER — ROBOTICS INTEGRATION & DEPLOYMENT

Be the person who makes advanced autonomous systems work in the real world. Move across the full stack: drones, sensors, calibration, autonomy software, perception, networking, payloads, field operations, and partner environments. When the system is not mission-ready, you create clarity, find the root cause, and help make it robust.

 


ABOUT SE3

SE3 Labs is building Spatial AI by combining 3D computer vision with large language models. We believe the ability to reason about and act within physical space is foundational to unlocking the next generation of automation, from construction to smart infrastructure, and ultimately, defence.

Now, we’re bringing Spatial AI into the sky through high-performance UAVs that can perceive, localize, coordinate, and act in the real world. This requires more than strong algorithms and good hardware. It requires systems that can be configured, calibrated, tested, deployed, demonstrated, debugged, and improved under real field conditions.

We’re looking for a hands-on engineer who wants to work close to the product, close to the field, and close to the full autonomy stack. Someone who is not limited to one layer, but follows the problem wherever it leads.

 


ABOUT THE ROLE

As (Senior) Technical Field Engineer — Robotics Integration & Deployment, you will own the last mile between SE3’s engineering team and real-world use. You will bring together UAVs, payloads, sensors, onboard compute, autonomy software, operator tools, and partner environments into systems that are ready to fly, test, demonstrate, and deploy.

This is a highly hands-on, full-stack field engineering role. When something is not working, you go where the evidence leads: recalibrate a sensor, solve an issue in the perception stack, debug a radio link, inspect a harness, analyse a flight log, adapt an autonomy behavior, fix a configuration issue, improve a checklist, or bring the right evidence back to the engineering team.

The problems you will work on are complex, dynamic, and multivariable. You will often have partial information, limited time, imperfect tools, and a system where hardware, software, environment, and operator behavior interact in non-obvious ways. Your job is to create clarity, choose the right debugging path, and drive the system toward a robust long-term solution.

You will work closely with perception, robotics software, embedded, hardware, and flight-test teams. You will not just operate systems; you will make them better. Your work will determine how quickly SE3 moves from prototype to repeatable deployment, from demo to product, and from individual systems to operational fleets.

 


WHAT YOU’LL WORK ON

 - Create Clarity in complex situations: Separate symptoms from root causes, know when to trust intuition and when not to, devise efficient debugging workflows, reproduce failures, analyse logs, and drive lasting solutions that make the system more robust long term.

 - Cross-Stack Engineering: Move across hardware, software, sensors, calibration, perception, networking, autonomy, and operator workflows. When the root cause requires it, contribute directly to the layer that matters, from sensor calibration and deployment configuration to perception-stack behavior, autonomy logic, networking, or hardware integration.

 - Autonomy & System Integration: Bring together UAVs, payloads, onboard compute, cameras, IMUs, radios, ground stations, autonomy software, operator interfaces, and deployment tooling into field-ready systems. Configure and validate perception-stack behavior, mission logic, autonomy behaviors, operator workflows, and system responses under real operating conditions.

 - Tooling, Automation & AI Workflows: Build and use intelligent debugging, analysis, and agent-based workflows to understand complex system behavior faster. Improve scripts, checklists, dashboards, log pipelines, diagnostics, configur

source_jobs.title: Procurement & Supply Chain Specialist for Drones & Embedded Electronics
source_jobs.description:
PROCUREMENT & SUPPLY CHAIN SPECIALIST FOR DRONES & EMBEDDED ELECTRONICS

Own the supplier network, logistics and trade-compliance backbone that gets our autonomy hardware built and fielded. Your work will help strengthen Europe’s defense with real capability.



ABOUT SE3

SE3 Labs is building Spatial AI by combining 3D computer vision with large language models (LLMs). We believe the ability to reason about and act within physical space is foundational to unlocking the next generation of automation, from construction to smart infrastructure, and ultimately, defense.

Now, we’re building the physical platform to bring Spatial AI to life. That means high-performance, real-world robotic systems (starting with UAVs) and we’re looking for a skilled, hands-on procurement specialist who’s ready to power the foundation of these systems and have them communicate, update and perform reliably in the real world.



ABOUT THE ROLE

As Procurement & Supply Chain Specialist, you’ll own sourcing, procurement and material planning for the hardware behind our UAV and defense systems. From standard parts and COTS electronics to designed components and contract-manufactured assemblies, you’ll make sure the right parts arrive on time, on quality and fully compliant.

You’ll collaborate closely with colleagues developing electronics, software, and AI together pushing to deliver vertically integrated and well-balanced solutions. Supply resilience, supplier performance, logistics and import/export execution will be your domain, and as the first dedicated owner of this function, you’ll be trusted to build the systems and supplier base we scale on. This is an on-site role with occasional travel to suppliers and partners.




WHAT YOU’LL WORK ON

 - Procure & Plan: Coordinate and execute procurement plus material planning for standard, COTS and designed parts across electronics, sensors, mechanical components and assemblies

 - Source the Best Options: Identify and acquire best-in-market components by translating engineering requirements into structured supplier choices, RFQs and trade-off decisions

 - Own Supplier Execution: Hold suppliers, contractors and contract manufacturers accountable for on-time, on-quality delivery and drive issues through to resolution

 - Build Resilience: Strengthen our European supply chain through on-shoring, second-sourcing, forward-thinking contracting and stockpiling

 - Handle Logistics & Compliance: Organize shipping and external logistics while ensuring compliant handling of customs, end-use declarations, permits, BAFA-related topics and Incoterms




YOUR PROFILE

 - You bring 2+ years of end-to-end experience in supply chain management for embedded systems, ideally in avionics, UAVs or similarly demanding hardware

 - You’re skilled to rank sensors, electronics and other technical components against engineering specs and practical system needs

 - You communicate clearly and effectively across engineering, suppliers, logistics partners and contractors

 - You’re highly dependable in how you structure projects, procurement flows and data

 - You’re familiar with contract manufacturing, BAFA-relevant workflows and Incoterms

 - You speak fluent English; German is a strong plus

 - Most importantly, you’re structured, proactive and mission-driven. You don’t wait for perfect conditions. You build resilient systems that keep critical hardware moving.




ESPECIALLY VALUED BONUS SKILLS

 - Prior experience with export regulations for dual-use and military goods

 - Ability to read and interpret technical drawings and manufacturing documentation

 - Strong negotiation skills with a holistic approach balancing pricing goals with supplier relations, quality and long-term needs

 - Familiarity with ERP systems, supplier scorecards or defense / aerospace supply chains




PERKS & BENEFITS

 - Work Environment to Excel at: Find a high-quality and modern toolset, flat hierarchy and a skilled, driven team which will push the l

notes: High-tech startup with strong focus on autonomous systems and Spatial AI. Most roles are technical, with a few non-technical positions in support functions.
